note: HOTFIX: auto-split was misclassifying mature coinbase UTXOs as immature on every wallet, locking out anything with mining history from the auto-split flow. Root cause: autoSplitCapsuleSend resolved chain height by reading parseInt(getElementById('balanceBlock').textContent) — there is no element with that id in the wallet, so getElementById returned null, .textContent threw, the try/catch returned 0, and chainHeight became 0. With chainHeight=0 the maturity check confs = 0 - height becomes negative, confs >= 1000 is always false, and every coinbase UTXO drops out of the spendable set. Non-coinbase UTXOs survive because the filter shortcuts via !u.coinbase. The smoking gun: a wallet with explorer-confirmed 10 726.55 SOST spendable across 2716 mature UTXOs saw the auto-split panel report 80.55 SOST spendable across 4 UTXOs (exactly the four non-coinbase entries) with the remaining 2740 misclassified as immature. Fix: refreshBalance now caches the authoritative height into window._lastChainHeight (same source the dashboard already uses, getinfo.blocks). autoSplitCapsuleSend reads from the cache; if the cache is missing or non-finite it makes a fresh getinfo RPC; if that still fails it throws a clear error before touching UTXO classification ('auto-split cannot determine chain height; refresh the wallet ... and retry'). Number/Number.isFinite guard so any NaN / undefined / non-positive gets caught instead of silently degrading. The diagnostic panel now shows the chain height that was used so any future regression of this class is visible at a glance ('Chain height used: #N · coinbase needs height − mined ≥ 1000 to be spendable.'). The single-recipient sendTransaction and sendmany paths already call rpcCall('getinfo') directly and were never affected; this commit touches only autoSplitCapsuleSend's chain-height resolution and refreshBalance's cache write. NO consensus, NO RPC schema, NO node, NO miner, NO transaction wire format.
